newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental news since June 6. The -12.1% June 9 drop (following a -7.5% session) is profit-taking / momentum unwind after the stock surged ~40% to fresh 52-week highs on Q2 guidance (+10-13% QoQ revenue, +160bps GM), a July dividend, and smart-glasses/CPO hype (FOCI partnership, Hunterbrook 'stealth NVIDIA optics supplier' narrative). TipRanks characterizes it as a company-specific cooldown with shares well above the ~$8 average analyst price target; no downgrade, contract loss, or guidance change found.

sources.label: TipRanks - Himax Stock Suddenly Sinks After Big Rally
Simply Wall St - HIMX valuation after upbeat guidance, new projects and dividend
Hunterbrook - Himax: Stealth Supplier for NVIDIA's Optics Revolution
GlobeNewswire - Himax confirms FOCI CPO collaboration on track for 2026 production

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict was removal for weak direct CPO/bottleneck relevance; nothing since June 6 changes that. Today's -12.1% flow-driven flush relieves some crowding froth, but a de-crowding sell-off in a name whose page thesis is 'low CPO exposure' does not create page-specific alpha, so hold at the bottom of the list with a token positive delta.
verdicts.bull: FOCI/WLO co-packaged optics partnership is on track for 2026 mass production with key customers validating gen-1 solutions, and the Hunterbrook NVIDIA-optics-supplier thesis gives genuine optionality the page score may undercount.
verdicts.neutral: CPO is still a small, pre-revenue slice of a display-driver company; the two-day ~18% flush takes out momentum froth but leaves the +109.5% YTD rerating largely intact.
verdicts.bear: Stock trades far above the ~$8 average analyst target after a retail-driven run; if CPO validation slips or the NVIDIA narrative deflates, the residual downside is large for a display-cycle business.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Confirmed CPO mass-production orders or a named tier-1 customer for the FOCI/WLO solution in 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Momentum unwind continues as the retail CPO narrative deflates while core display-driver fundamentals cannot support the rerated multiple.

verdicts.rankRationale: Edge-AI vision/display thesis is unchanged and fundamentals are intact (Q2 guide +10-13% QoQ, top smart-glasses brand mass production by end-2026), so the -12.1% flow-driven sell-off modestly improves the entry without changing the scaling-fit rank set yesterday at hold.
verdicts.bull: Smart-glasses microdisplays (Vuzix reference design, unnamed top-brand ramp by end-2026), automotive Tcon double-digit sequential growth, and CPO optionality give multiple edge-AI scaling vectors; the two-day flush resets a crowded entry.
verdicts.neutral: No new evidence since June 6; the move is sentiment, not thesis, and the name still carries a +109.5% YTD rerating against a $3.1B cap.
verdicts.bear: Core revenue remains display-driver cyclical (Q1 revenue -2% QoQ); if the smart-glasses ramp slips, the edge-AI narrative premium compresses hard from here.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: End-2026 mass production of the top-tier smart-glasses program and Q2 results confirming the 10-13% QoQ guide.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Display-cycle softness or a smart-glasses program delay turns the momentum unwind into a fundamental derating.
